etymology.description: 言(言) (yán, "words; to speak") + 八(八) (bā, "divide; breath coming from the mouth") + 厃(厃) (wěi, "danger") -> speaking too much
etymology.interpretation: The character 詹(詹) (zhān, "prolong") combines 言(言) (yán, "words; to speak") with 八(八) (bā, "breath coming from the mouth") and 厃(厃) (wěi, "danger") to convey the idea of talking extensively or excessively, suggesting that excessive speech might lead to danger or problems.

parts.english: divide; breath coming from the mouth
